dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes entering after trend has shifted and price has moved in new direction for minimum 2 bars. Entry conditions use price crossing above/below the 10-period SMA (trend confirmation) combined with price moving in desired direction (close > prev_close for long, close < prev_close for short). Exit triggers on trend reversal (price crossing back through SMA). The 5-bar lookback parameter for prior trend direction is implicit in the cross logic. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the rule applies equally to long and short entries based on which way the trend has shifted.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal emphasizes entering after trend has shifted and price has moved in new direction for minimum 2 bars. Entry conditions use price crossing above/below the 10-period SMA (trend confirmation) combined with price moving in desired direction (close > prev_close for long, close < prev_close for short). Exit triggers on trend reversal (price crossing back through SMA). The 5-bar lookback parameter for prior trend direction is implicit in the cross logic. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the rule applies equally to long and short entries based on which way the trend has shifted.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter a position only after the trend has already shifted and begun moving in the desired direction, not in anticipation of a trend change.
